summary: Iteration 279 — Verified extended deletion-audit trail (SHIPMENT delete + INVOICE VOID) and delivery DELETE→RESTORE round-trip. 8/8 pytest cases passed. Confirmed: (1) DELETE /api/distributors/{did}/shipments/{sid} on a synthetic DRAFT shipment (+2 items) as CEO removes the shipment/items and writes exactly one deletion_audit row with entity_type='shipment', entity_number=shipment_number, item_count=2, items_snapshot without _id, tenant_id, distributor_id, deleted_by_email=CEO, deleted_at ISO — and appears in GET /{did}/deletion-audit?entity_type=shipment. (2) POST /api/invoices/{id}/void on a synthetic 'matched' invoice writes deletion_audit row with entity_type='invoice_void', entity_number=invoice_no, deleted_by_email=CEO, deleted_at ISO, zoho_voided flag, snapshot without _id; the invoice is deleted; the row appears in GET /distributors/deletion-audit/all?entity_type=invoice_void. Guards intact: wrong confirmation → 400 'Type VOID', bogus id → 404. (3) DELETE→RESTORE: after CEO delete, POST /{did}/deletion-audit/{aid}/restore returns success=true, delivery is back in distributor_deliveries with SAME id/delivery_number, both items are back in distributor_delivery_items, the restored delivery has restored_at + restored_by=CEO email, and the audit row is marked restored_at/restored_by. (4) Restore guards all pass: (a) re-restoring same audit_id → 400 'already been restored'; (b) restoring a non-delivery audit (entity_type='shipment') → 400 'Only deleted deliveries can be restored'; (c) distributor user → 403 'Only CEO/Admin can restore deleted records'; (d) bogus audit_id → 404; (e) if a delivery with the snapshot id already exists → 400 'already exists'. (5) Regression — GET /deletion-audit/all and per-distributor /deletion-audit still return {total, records[]} correctly. All synthetic TEST-* docs & audit rows cleaned up.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/invoices.py L1017: deletion_audit insert for invoice_void is wrapped in try/except → logs and continues (good — never blocks void). However unlike routes/distributors.py, it does NOT go through _record_deletion_audit(), so the field-shape is hand-rolled and can drift. Suggest consolidating into a shared helper (e.g. move _record_deletion_audit out of routes/distributors.py into a common module and reuse it here) so schema stays consistent.

routes/distributors.py L5623: role check uses substring match — `any(r in user_role for r in ['ceo','admin','system admin'])`. A future custom role that contains the word 'admin' (e.g. 'admin_readonly') would inadvertently gain restore rights. Prefer exact-match against a canonical role set (same pattern used in is_distributor_admin()).

routes/distributors.py L5649: restore inserts snapshot back into db.distributor_deliveries but does NOT reconstruct any downstream side-effects (stock ledger, applied credit notes, Zoho mirror). The docstring says this is by design (delete didn't reverse them either). Please document this behaviour on the API/UI side so CEO/Admin know they may need to re-run reconciliation if the deletion actually reversed stock somewhere else.

routes/invoices.py L1015 comment says 'Record deletion audit ... before removing the local doc' but the invoice's outstanding-balance decrement (step 2, L1007) already ran before the audit write, so the snapshot captured has outstanding_counted=true even though the account has already been adjusted. Minor: if we ever want the snapshot to be a 'perfect' pre-void copy for restore, move the audit write earlier.
test_credentials: CEO: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (nyla-air-water); Low-priv: john.distributor@test.com / nyladist##
seed_data_creation: All synthetic — created directly via MONGO_URL for each test and cleaned up in finally-blocks: (a) 1 draft shipment + 2 items in distributor_shipments/distributor_shipment_items; (b) 1 'matched' invoice with EMPTY zoho_invoice_id in invoices; (c) 2 draft deliveries + 2 items each in distributor_deliveries/distributor_delivery_items (used for restore round-trip and 403 low-priv test); (d) a couple of hand-crafted deletion_audit rows for the 'non-delivery' and 'already exists' guard tests. A housekeeping test purges any TEST- prefixed rows at the end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Extended audit fields: shipment audits carry item_count + items_snapshot; invoice_void audits carry zoho_voided flag + reason. Restore endpoint writes restored_at/restored_by both on the audit row AND on the reinserted delivery. IMPORTANT (mocking note): the reviewer asked us to monkeypatch services.zoho_service.void_invoice, but we're testing via the external URL and cannot monkeypatch a running process from pytest. Instead we exercise the same code path by seeding the synthetic invoice with an EMPTY zoho_invoice_id — services.zoho_service.void_invoice already short-circuits with `if not zoho_invoice_id: return True` (see zoho_service.py L1114), so NO live Zoho call is made. This is functionally equivalent to a no-op monkeypatch. The `zoho_voided` audit field is therefore captured as False in this test; if the main agent wants to test the True branch, they'd need to run inside the backend process (pytest fixture with monkeypatch on services.zoho_service.void_invoice) rather than via HTTP.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature verification only. All requested behaviours confirmed exactly as described in the review request.
